h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to continue placing and naming the four label cards in the same top-to-bottom order established from the start.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: sets the overlay aside and places the cards on the tile
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner performs the placement task without the overlay scaffold for the first time, which is a modest actional step forward, but the verbal output is nearly identical to L3, so the action shows consolidation rather than a meaningfully new device or criterion.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Now one slot has no answer. The symbol space holds C, but the overlay still asks two questions: which number names the element, and which number gives the mass entry? Put one number back, then name the job of 6.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T1 introduces a mild disruption by removing a card to create a visible gap, which is a small representational shift, but it does not show explicit stock-taking contrast against a failed prior route, and the subsequent tutor turns (T2–T4) continue the same overlay-and-label route without inventing a changed mechanism, so neither part A nor part B of the reversal criterion is fully met.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Now one slot has no answer. The symbol space holds C, but the overlay still asks two questions: which number names the element, and which number gives the mass entry? Put one number back, then name the job of 6.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The device of removing one card to expose an empty slot is a minor variation on the same labeling route rather than a precision-fitted new mechanism; it reduces cognitive load slightly but does not give the learner a transferable criterion or evidence standard that addresses a genuine blockage.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so recognition-contingent tutor uptake cannot occur; all tutor turns follow the pre-established lesson plan without adapting to any revised learner framing.
